source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
reason: Requires a second agent CLI (codex/pi/aider) running in parallel with claudecode in the same cwd. The current ir:onboard-agent driver orchestrates one CLI at a time per recipe; cross-adapter parallel orchestration is not yet implemented. Agent capability and irrlicht observability are both fine — see assessment.json — but no recording can be produced as-shipped. This cell is also a dispatcher test that lives equally on the claudecode and codex rows of the matrix; once the driver supports paired adapters, the same fixture serves both.
notes: Spec preserved for forward compatibility: one claudecode session + one codex (or pi) session in the same cwd, each observed under its correct adapter label with independent state transitions and no cross-contamination of metrics, parent linkage, or subagent counts. Future phases (when driver supports cross-adapter parallel orchestration): claudecode_birth (adapter:claudecode, ready), codex_birth (adapter:codex, ready), each adapter's own turn arc, invariants on both: no leakage of subagent counts or parent_linked across adapter boundaries (asserted by adapter name on the SessionState row, not by cwd). Driver extension blocked: needs a `parallel_adapter` step type and matrix support for paired-CLI recipes.
